notes: v341 — CHECKED THE MATHS, AND UPGRADED RSI TO THE STANDARD. You asked me to verify the calculations. I went through the core formulas and they're sound: the volume-surge %, the range position, the /10 watch score (it's a clean weighted score that caps at 10), the accumulation count (its baseline correctly excludes the days it's counting so it can't flatter itself), the volume average (correctly excludes today so today's surge is measured against a clean baseline), and the report card's honesty maths — the “edge” is a proper market-adjusted average (it subtracts each day's whole-market move before scoring, so a strong market week can't flatter a flag), with a correct t-statistic behind the SOLID/PROMISING tiers. One thing I improved: the RSI indicator was using a simple 14-day average, which is a valid variant but not what broker charts show. I switched it to Wilder's smoothing — the standard RSI every charting platform uses — so the number now matches what you'd see elsewhere (checked against the textbook reference). Its overbought/oversold (70/30) reading and the 🪜 Pullback scan's “RSI cooled” step now read conventionally. Nothing you do changes; the figures are just more standard and correct. Hard-refresh to load v341. ALSO RECENT — v340 — the 📊 Range column is back (a stale responsive rule had been hiding it). ALSO RECENT — v339 — ↩ Undo now sticks (restored positions aren't instantly re-sold). ALSO RECENT — v338 — Undo auto-sells + tidier share row. (Older notes live in the Owner's Manual changelog.)
